description: A small real-paper claim audit database: CAR-T trial claims with PubMed/DOI provenance, verbatim evidence spans, scope conditions, safety-risk flags, and distillation-gate promotion decisions.

source_papers.title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma

demo_queries.query: What efficacy and safety claims are supported for the CAR-T trials?
demo_queries.expected_behavior: Return efficacy claims with trial scope, and include safety-risk claims instead of only reporting response rates.

demo_queries.query: Which promoted CAR-T claims are safety risks?
demo_queries.expected_behavior: Return CRS, neurologic events, cytopenias, adverse events, and deaths.

records.paper_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
records.text: Tisagenlecleucel produced an 81% overall remission rate within 3 months.
scope_conditions: CD19+ relapsed or refractory B-cell ALL
records.evidence_span: The overall remission rate within 3 months was 81%

claim.text: Tisagenlecleucel produced an 81% overall remission rate within 3 months.

records.text: All responders were negative for minimal residual disease by flow cytometry.

records.paper_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
records.text: Event-free and overall survival were reported at 6 and 12 months.
records.evidence_span: The rates of event-free survival and overall survival were 73% (95% confidence interval [CI], 60 to 82) and 90% (95% CI, 81 to 95), respectively, at 6 months and 50% (95% CI, 35 to 64) and 76% (95% CI, 63 to 86) at 12 months.

claim.text: Event-free and overall survival were reported at 6 and 12 months.

claim.evidence_span: The rates of event-free survival and overall survival were 73% (95% confidence interval [CI], 60 to 82) and 90% (95% CI, 81 to 95), respectively, at 6 months and 50% (95% CI, 35 to 64) and 76% (95% CI, 63 to 86) at 12 months.

records.text: Cytokine release syndrome occurred in 77% of patients.
records.evidence_span: The cytokine release syndrome occurred in 77% of patients, 48% of whom received tocilizumab.

records.paper_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
records.text: Neurologic events occurred in 40% of patients and no cerebral edema was reported.
records.evidence_span: Neurologic events occurred in 40% of patients and were managed with supportive care, and no cerebral edema was reported.

records.paper_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
records.text: Tisagenlecleucel was generated from autologous T cells transduced with a lentiviral vector expressing CAR signaling domains.
records.evidence_span: Tisagenlecleucel was generated ex vivo with the use of autologous T cells transduced with a lentiviral vector to express a CAR containing a CD3-zeta domain to provide a T-cell activation signal and a 4-1BB (CD137) domain to provide a costimulatory signal.

claim.text: Tisagenlecleucel was generated from autologous T cells transduced with a lentiviral vector expressing CAR signaling domains.

records.paper_title: Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma
records.text: At a median follow-up of 15.4 months, 42% of patients continued to have a response.

records.paper_title: Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma
records.text: Grade 3 or higher neutropenia, anemia, and thrombocytopenia were common during treatment.
records.evidence_span: The most common adverse events of grade 3 or higher during treatment were neutropenia (in 78% of the patients), anemia (in 43%), and thrombocytopenia (in 38%).
